forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8180'
+project: claude-hooks
+title: 'Matcher fidelity: exact-string vs regex with full-match anchoring'
+---
+`HookConfig::build_registrations` (crates/agent-client-protocol-extras/src/hook_config.rs) compiles every non-empty, non-`*` matcher as a raw `regex::Regex` and uses `is_match` (unanchored substring). This diverges from Claude Code's documented matcher rules:
+
+- `"*"`, `""`, or omitted → match all.
+- Matcher containing only `[A-Za-z0-9_|]` → treated as an exact string, or `|`-separated list of exact strings (e.g. `Edit|Write`). Must be a FULL match, not substring.
+- Matcher containing any other character → JavaScript-style regex, anchored to a full match.
+
+Today `matcher: "Bash"` incorrectly matches a tool named `Bash2` or `xBash` (substring). `Edit|Write` happens to work as regex alternation but is unanchored.
+
+## Scope
+- Update matcher construction/`HookRegistration::matches` so plain identifier matchers compare for full-string equality (split on `|` for alternation) and regex matchers are anchored (wrap as `^(?:...)$`).
+- Keep the `Option<regex::Regex>` storage or introduce a small `Matcher` enum (Exact set vs Regex vs All) — implementer's call; keep it internal to the crate.
+- Preserve existing event-without-matcher behavior (UserPromptSubmit/Stop always fire).
+
+## Acceptance criteria + tests
+- `Bash` matches only `Bash`, not `Bash2`/`xBash`/`run-Bash`.
+- `Edit|Write` matches `Edit` and `Write` exactly, not `Editor`.
+- `mcp__memory__.*` (regex) matches `mcp__memory__create_entities`; `^Notebook` matches `NotebookEdit`.
+- `*` and empty match everything.
+- All existing hook_config tests still pass.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -24,4 +24,15 @@
 - `Edit|Write` matches `Edit` and `Write` exactly, not `Editor`.
 - `mcp__memory__.*` (regex) matches `mcp__memory__create_entities`; `^Notebook` matches `NotebookEdit`.
 - `*` and empty match everything.
-- All existing hook_config tests still pass.
\ No newline at end of file
+- All existing hook_config tests still pass.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-04 13:55)
+
+Adjudication of the two flagged items:
+1. **Anchoring contradiction — implementer is correct.** The Scope note ("anchored, wrap as `^(?:...)$`") and the acceptance criterion (`^Notebook` matches `NotebookEdit`) are genuinely mutually exclusive: wrapping `^Notebook` as `^(?:^Notebook)$` can never match `NotebookEdit`. Unanchored JS `RegExp.test`-style semantics is the right call — it satisfies the concrete acceptance examples and matches real Claude Code behavior (`new RegExp(matcher).test(toolName)`). No change needed; the acceptance criteria are the authoritative spec.
+2. **Public API change — acceptable and internally consistent.** `HookRegistration.matcher: Option<regex::Regex>` → `Matcher` was explicitly authorized by the task. `Matcher` is `pub` and re-exported via `lib.rs`; it must be public because the public `HookRegistration::new`/`with_registration`/`with_hook` take it. All callers in `hookable_agent.rs` are migrated. The Scope's "keep it internal to the crate" is not literally met (the enum is exported), but exporting is unavoidable given the public constructors, so this is fine.
+
+All 5 acceptance criteria verified by passing tests (218 passed, clippy clean).
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/agent-client-protocol-extras/src/hookable_agent.rs` — The `with_hook` doc comment describes regex matchers as a "full-match-anchored regex", directly contradicting the actual unanchored JS-`test` behavior (and the authoritative `Matcher` enum doc in `hook_config.rs`, which correctly says "intentionally unanchored, so the pattern author controls anchoring via `^`/`$`"). A config author reading this method's docs would wrongly expect `mcp__memory__.*` to require a full match. Fix: change "anything else is a full-match-anchored regex" to match the unanchored JS-`test` semantics actually implemented.
\ No newline at end of file

 - `mcp__memory__.*` (regex) matches `mcp__memory__create_entities`; `^Notebook` matches `NotebookEdit`.
 - `*` and empty match everything.

-
-## Review Findings (2026-06-04 13:55)
-
-Adjudication of the two flagged items:
-1. **Anchoring contradiction — implementer is correct.** The Scope note ("anchored, wrap as `^(?:...)$`") and the acceptance criterion (`^Notebook` matches `NotebookEdit`) are genuinely mutually exclusive: wrapping `^Notebook` as `^(?:^Notebook)$` can never match `NotebookEdit`. Unanchored JS `RegExp.test`-style semantics is the right call — it satisfies the concrete acceptance examples and matches real Claude Code behavior (`new RegExp(matcher).test(toolName)`). No change needed; the acceptance criteria are the authoritative spec.
-2. **Public API change — acceptable and internally consistent.** `HookRegistration.matcher: Option<regex::Regex>` → `Matcher` was explicitly authorized by the task. `Matcher` is `pub` and re-exported via `lib.rs`; it must be public because the public `HookRegistration::new`/`with_registration`/`with_hook` take it. All callers in `hookable_agent.rs` are migrated. The Scope's "keep it internal to the crate" is not literally met (the enum is exported), but exporting is unavoidable given the public constructors, so this is fine.
-
-All 5 acceptance criteria verified by passing tests (218 passed, clippy clean).
-
-### Warnings
-- [ ] `crates/agent-client-protocol-extras/src/hookable_agent.rs` — The `with_hook` doc comment describes regex matchers as a "full-match-anchored regex", directly contradicting the actual unanchored JS-`test` behavior (and the authoritative `Matcher` enum doc in `hook_config.rs`, which correctly says "intentionally unanchored, so the pattern author controls anchoring via `^`/`$`"). A config author reading this method's docs would wrongly expect `mcp__memory__.*` to require a full match. Fix: change "anything else is a full-match-anchored regex" to match the unanchored JS-`test` semantics actually implemented.
\ No newline at end of file
+- All existing hook_config tests still pass.
